meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve no version control (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no version control. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Mastering User Onboarding Without Version Control
content.seo_description: Learn to implement robust user onboarding while navigating version control gaps. An expert guide for students using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: To overcome the chaos of coding without version control, implement a structured user onboarding flow that acts as a state-management anchor. By using Claude Code to automate documentation and Antigravity to scaffold your user journey, you create a repeatable, reliable development experience that compensates for manual file management.
content.intro:
You are staring at a folder of 'final_v2_new.js' files, heart racing as you realize you have no way to roll back your last deployment. The terror of the 'no version control' trap is the rite of passage for every student, but it is also the greatest barrier to shipping professional-grade software. You feel like you are building on sand, waiting for the tide to wash away your progress.

But here is the secret: stability isn't found in a repo, it is found in the architecture of your user experience. By mastering user onboarding, you force yourself to build modular, state-driven features that are easier to manage even when your git history is non-existent. Let's turn your lack of infrastructure into your greatest architectural advantage.

steps.title: Define the Onboarding State Machine
steps.description: Before writing code, map out the user journey as a sequence of discrete states. This prevents logic leaks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a state machine diagram for a 3-step user onboarding flow that handles initial profile setup, tool walkthrough, and final confirmation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude create --file onboarding_schema.json --context 'Define state transitions for user onboarding'

steps.title: Build the Skeleton with Antigravity
steps.description: Use Antigravity to scaffold the component structure, ensuring your onboarding flow is decoupled from your main application logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create React components for a modular onboarding wizard. Ensure each step is a separate file to minimize risk of overwriting code.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Scaffold onboarding components using the generated schema'

steps.title: Implement Persistent State Persistence
steps.description: Since you lack version control, save onboarding progress to LocalStorage. This allows you to recover state if you accidentally overwrite a file.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a utility hook that saves the current onboarding step to localStorage and retrieves it upon page load.

steps.title: Automate Documentation with Claude
steps.description: Use Claude Code to document your current build state. This acts as a manual versioning log.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a summary file that documents the current onboarding functionality and dependencies.

steps.title: Validate via Simulation
steps.description: Simulate user interactions to ensure the onboarding flow doesn't break under edge-case inputs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate test cases for the onboarding flow covering completion, cancellation, and mid-session refresh.

pro_tips: Always use date-stamped folder names for your project daily to simulate versioning without git.
Keep your onboarding logic in a single folder so you can quickly zip it up as a snapshot.
Use Claude Code to 'diff' your current code against your last documented state file.
Treat your onboarding flow as a 'living documentation'—if you can't explain the flow to a user, your code is too complex.
Avoid global state libraries until you have mastered local component state; it reduces the blast radius of bugs.
content.next_steps: Once your onboarding flow is stable, your next move is to learn basic Git integration. Use this onboarding module as your first commit in a real repository to transition from student to professional developer.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the strategy to turn your project into a portfolio piece that recruiters will actually notice. Stop letting version control fears hold you back from shipping. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
